message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~999 reviews + 1,162 photos; TripAdvisor ~350 reviews; Google Maps cited as 300+ positive reviews (combined ~1,500+ across platforms)
- Sentiment: mostly positive (4.3/5 on TripAdvisor) with mixed recent signals
- Longevity: Opened 2012 by father-son team Mark and Drew Davis; ~14 years operating. Late-2024 ownership/management change referenced in recent reviews [UNVERIFIED specifics of new owner identity]
- Credentials: ["TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice (referenced in TripAdvisor profile)","TripAdvisor #5-#7 of ~68-80 Rancho Mirage restaurants","Featured in Palm Springs Life (2013 chef profile)","Featured in Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, Coachella Valley Weekly","Gayot listing","Desert Business Association member","Chef Drew Davis trained under James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello in Miami; CIA alum; former Exec Sous-Chef at Nick & Toni's (East Hampton); former private chef to Steven Spielberg"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best Spanish restaurant Coachella Valley/Palm Springs', 'tapas restaurant near Palm Springs Iberico ham octopus', 'best paella Coachella Valley', 'Spanish restaurants Rancho Mirage'. Surfaces weakly or not at all for: 'date night Rancho Mirage' (loses to The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Shame on the Moon, Willie's Modern Fare), 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' (omitted from Palm Springs Life and OpenTable curated date-night lists), and generic 'best restaurants Rancho Mirage' top-10 mentions (typically a lower-third mention rather than headline).
- Site structured data: Weak. catalanrestaurants.com is built on GoDaddy Website Builder. NO schema.org / JSON-LD / Restaurant markup detected. Street address is not in visible body content (phone only). Hours are presented as plain text. Menu pages exist but are not semantically marked. Social links to Facebook/Instagram/Yelp present; no OpenTable widget embedded; no online ordering. A second domain (catalanrestaurant.com) hosts an About page, suggesting possible domain split/duplication post-ownership-change.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here are the search results for "Catalan" restaurant in Rancho Mirage with Mark Davis and Drew Davis:

Catalan was launched by a father and son duo, Mark and Drew Davis. Catalan is a Mediterranean themed restaurant located on Highway 111 in Rancho Mirage, in the building where Bangkok Five once was.

**About the Owners:**
- Mark Davis is the patriarch of the Davis family, who was living his retirement out in the Coachella Valley after a successful career in the shipping industry.
- Chef Drew Davis had a passion for food from childhood, attended the Culinary Institute of America, worked under James Beard awarded chefs in Florida and the Midwest, and most recently worked as Executive Sous-Chef at Nick and Toni's in East Hampton, New York. Drew also worked as a private chef to Steven Spielberg before deciding to open a restaurant in the Palm Springs area.

**Restaurant Details:**
Catalan in Rancho Mirage opened in 2012. The owners source their products exclusively from local and sustainable sources whenever they can in a commitment to give their patrons a farm to table dining experience.

Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant is located in the heart of Rancho Mirage and serves a variety of Spanish-infused dishes in a rustic yet modern setting. The family-owned and operated business has been providing the desert culinary scene with one of the finest Tapas & Paella experiences since 2012.

**Opening and Background:**
The restaurant had just opened prior to the 2012 edition. Catalan is a Mediterranean-themed restaurant located in the building where Bangkok Five once was on Highway 111 and was launched by a father and son duo, Mark and Drew Davis.

**Recognition:**
The combination has created a Mediterranean cuisine that captured a 2012 Diner's Choice nod from OpenTable.com.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mark Militello:

James Beard Award-winning chef Mark Militello is often credited with putting South Florida on America's culinary map. He received a James Beard Award for "Best Regional Chef" in the Southeast, as well as two "Golden Dish" awards from GQ magazine and a "Distinguished Restaurant Award" from Conde Nast Traveler.

Militello is recognized for creating contemporary cuisine that has Italian, Asian and Caribbean influences. Militello opened his first restaurant, Mark's Place, in North Miami Beach, Florida in 1988 and Mark's in the Grove. Just two years later, Food & Wine named him one of the "10 Best Chefs in America." In ensuing years, he operated four other namesake restaurants in South Florida: Mark's Las Olas in Fort Lauderdale, Mark's Mizner Park in Boca Raton, Mark's South Beach on Miami Beach and Mark's City Place in West Palm Beach.

Locally, Militello is revered as one of the "Mango Gang" – a group of Miami area chefs who revolutionized the South Florida dining landscape in the late 1980s. Currently, Militello is a restaurant consultant and is providing his expertise to Josie's Ristorante in Boynton Beach, ZErdem in Greenport, NY and Blu Mar in Southhampton, NY.

# Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant - Rancho Mirage

**Review Count & Rating:**
- 350 reviews
- 4.3 out of 5 stars
- Ranked #5 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage

**Travelers' Choice Award:**
Yes, the restaurant displays a Travelers' Choice badge, indicating it "consistently earn[s] great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

**Ownership Changes:**
The page mentions recent management transition. Multiple reviews from late 2024 reference "new ownership," with one November 2024 review noting: "Under new ownership, this restaurant was a pleasant surprise" and praising "a charming couple at the helm." A December 2024 review also states "New ownership has taken over and we were really impressed."

**Notable Observations:**
Recent reviews show mixed experiences, with some praising improved quality under new management while others criticize portion sizes and ingredient substitutions in signature dishes like paella.

content.text: Eismann is described only as a "James Beard Best Chef Nominee" — that's nomination, not winner. The claim in the assessment says "James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello" — but Eismann appears only nominated, plus name is misspelled. Let me verify further.

content.content:

Web search results for query: ""Jonathan Eismann" "Pacific Time" Miami chef biography"

Links: [{"title":"Jonathan Eismann (Retired) - Great Chefs","url":"https://greatchefs.com/chef/jonathan-eismann-retired/"},{"title":"Jonathan Eismann, PA | JONATHAN EISMANN PA - | Miami, FL","url":"https://www.loopnet.com/commercial-real-estate-brokers/profile/jonathan-eismann-pa/qbfqxczb"},{"title":"Jonathan Eismann | Restaurants | Miami | Miami New Times | The Leading Independent News Source in Miami, Florida","url":"https://www.miaminewtimes.com/restaurants/jonathan-eismann-6382808"},{"title":"Jonathan Eismann — Gridline Properties | Full Service Commercial Real Estate Brokerage","url":"https://www.gridlineproperties.com/jonathan-eismann"},{"title":"Kitchen Inquisition: Jonathan Eismann – NBC 6 South Florida","url":"https://www.nbcmiami.com/local/kitchen-inquisition-jonathsn-eismann/1875450/"},{"title":"Jonathan Eismann: Using Food and Beverage in Urban Placemaking - Fried on Business","url":"https://friedonbusiness.com/jonathan-eismann-using-food-and-beverage-in-urban-placemaking/"},{"title":"Jonathan Eismann","url":"https://www.miaminewtimes.com/food-drink/jonathan-eismann-6382808/"}]

I've found comprehensive information about Jonathan Eismann and his iconic Pacific Time restaurant. Here's his biography:

## Early Career and Training

Born in 1959, Jonathan Eismann was always interested in Asian influenced food, particularly with a French sensibility. He graduated from the Culinary Institute of America as Student Voted Most Likely To Succeed and was honored as an Ambassador to the University in 2008.

After graduating from the CIA, he worked as a chef at Acute Café in New York City, then went on to head some of the hottest restaurants of the 1980's, including Baton's Fandango, Mondial, China Grill, and Busby's. At the age of 27, he was Executive Chef at the globally acclaimed China Grill Restaurant in NYC, where he managed a 165-person staff.

## Pacific Time Restaurant

When he opened Pacific Time with Yves Picot and Jean-Paul Picot in 1993, it took only one year to be recognized as one of the Best Restaurants in South Florida. He created and was Chef Owner of the world renowned Pacific Time Restaurant in Miami Beach for 17 years. Eismann's retired eatery, Pan-Asian icon Pacific Time, was credited with kick-starting Miami's resto culture.

## Later Career

He went on to open a second restaurant, Pacific Heights, in 1996 and several more thereafter, but an unfortunate accident in 2012 forced him to retire from the kitchens. Since then, Eismann has 25 years of experience in commercial real estate as both a successful principal investor and a top producing sales associate.

content.text:
I now have enough verification. Let me finalize:

- **Mark Militello**: confirmed James Beard winner (Best Regional Chef Southeast)
- **Jonathan Eismann**: only a nominee, not a winner — the claim says "James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman" — that's incorrect, and the spelling is wrong (Eismann, not Eisman)
- Father-son Mark + Drew Davis, opened 2012: confirmed
- CIA alum, Nick & Toni's exec sous-chef, private chef to Spielberg: confirmed in multiple press
- TripAdvisor #5-#7, 350 reviews, 4.3, Travelers' Choice: confirmed (#5 in current fetch, #7 in another; both within range)
- Yelp 999 reviews + 1162 photos: confirmed
- New ownership late 2024: confirmed in TripAdvisor reviews ("charming couple" referenced)
- Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, Coachella Valley Weekly, Gayot, Desert Business Association: confirmed via search results

confirms: Opened 2012 by father-son team Mark and Drew Davis (confirmed by Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, desert luxury real-estate coverage)
Chef Drew Davis CIA alum, former Exec Sous-Chef at Nick & Toni's East Hampton, former private chef to Steven Spielberg (confirmed in multiple press profiles)
Mark Militello is a verified James Beard Award winner (Best Regional Chef Southeast); Drew Davis training under Militello is plausible and consistent with press bios
TripAdvisor profile shows ~350 reviews, 4.3/5 rating, ranking #5 of 80 Rancho Mirage restaurants, and a Travelers' Choice badge
Yelp shows ~999 reviews and 1,162 photos (matches listing title in search results, June 2026)
Late-2024 ownership/management change is referenced in TripAdvisor reviews (November/December 2024 reviewers mention 'new ownership' and 'a charming couple at the helm')
Featured coverage exists in Palm Springs Life (2013), Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, Coachella Valley Weekly, Gayot listing, and Desert Business Association membership
contradicts: Claim that Drew Davis trained under 'James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello' is partly incorrect: (a) the name is spelled Jonathan Eismann (double-n), not Eisman; (b) Eismann is documented only as a James Beard Best Chef NOMINEE for Pacific Time in Miami Beach, not a winner — no James Beard win appears in his biography or in James Beard records I could surface. Only Militello is a confirmed Beard winner.
flagged_as_unverified: Identity of the specific new owner(s) post-late-2024 ownership change — reviews mention 'a charming couple' but no name was confirmed
Google Maps '300+ positive reviews' figure — not independently verified in this pass
Existence of a second domain catalanrestaurant.com hosting an About page — not independently fetched

Claim that Drew Davis trained specifically in Miami under Eismann — press bios say he 'worked under James Beard awarded chefs in Florida and the Midwest' without naming Miami or Eismann directly, so the Miami-specific framing is inferred rather than confirmed
Whether TripAdvisor rank is currently #5 vs #7 — search snippets show both; current live rank #5 of 80
input.notes: Operating history (2012 open date, father-son Mark/Drew Davis) and press credentials are solidly verified. Major refutation: Jonathan Eismann is a James Beard nominee, not winner, and his name is misspelled in the assessment — the phrase 'James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello' should be corrected to something like 'James Beard-recognized chefs (winner Mark Militello and nominee Jonathan Eismann).' Late-2024 ownership change is corroborated by review-text evidence but the new owners' identity remains unverified, which matters because brief language should not assert specifics about the new operators.
